hero.title: Learn to protect yourself.
hero.subtitle: Learn to protect yourself online. No jargon. No fear. Free.
hero.audience: Made for people who feel like tech wasn't built for them.

passwords.title: Secure passwords
passwords.description: Learn to create and manage passwords nobody can guess.

phishing.title: Detect phishing
phishing.description: Identify fake emails and sites before they trick you.

mfa.title: MFA authentication
mfa.description: Add an extra layer of protection to your accounts.

simulators.title: Simulators
simulators.description: Practice in safe environments that mimic real attacks.

step1.title: Pick a topic
step1.description: Choose the module that interests you most. No required order.

step2.title: Learn by doing
step2.description: Short lessons with interactive quizzes. You practice what you learn, right away.

step3.title: Practice in simulators
step3.description: Face simulated attacks in a 100% safe environment. No real risks.

simulator.heading: Practice without fear
simulator.subheading: Nothing you type is real. Learn to detect fake pages in a safe environment.

newsletter.heading: Stay up to date
newsletter.subheading: We'll let you know when there's new content. No spam.
newsletter.success: Done. We'll notify you when there's news.

follow.heading: Follow us where you already are
follow.subheading: We post on Instagram, TikTok, and X. Short carousels, real examples, no jargon.
follow.postsBanner: Coming soon: live posts from Instagram. For now, a sample of the kind of content.

post1.title: How to spot a fake email

post3.title: What is 2FA and why it matters

simulator.title: Simulator
simulator.subtitle: Practice in safe environments. Nothing you type is real.
simulator.banner: This is a training simulation. Nothing you type here is real or saved.

validation.emailRequired: Enter your email address to continue.
validation.mfaRequired: Enter the verification code to continue.
validation.noAccountWarning: Attention! A real site would validate your credentials here.

stub.title: Coming soon
stub.subtitle: This flow is being built in the next MVP phase.

phone.label: Recovery phone (optional)
phone.help: Helps you regain access if you lose your account. Never shown publicly.

password.label: Password
password.help: At least 8 characters. Mix letters, numbers and symbols.

name.title: Create your Google Account
name.subtitle: Enter your name
name.tip: Your name is visible across Google services. You don’t need to use your legal name.

birthday.title: When is your birthday?
birthday.subtitle: Google uses this to keep your account safe.
birthday.tip: Lying about your age can get accounts closed. Real platforms enforce age limits by law.

email.title: Pick your Gmail address
email.subtitle: We suggest one based on your name. You can edit it.
email.tip: Your email is visible to anyone you message. Don’t put sensitive info (like an ID number) in it.

password.title: Create a strong password
password.subtitle: Mix letters, numbers and symbols.
password.tip: Strong passwords are long, never reused, and not in any leak. A password manager helps a lot.

phone.title: Add a recovery phone
phone.subtitle: If you lose your password, this lets you regain access.
phone.tip: Without recovery contact, losing your password usually means losing the account forever.

review.title: Almost done
review.subtitle: By continuing, you accept the (simulated) terms of service.
review.tip: On real sites, read the terms — especially the privacy and data-use sections.

email.title: Sign in
email.tip: Always check the URL. Common phishing uses domains like gooogle.com or google-login.security.

password.title: Welcome back
password.subtitle: Enter your password to continue.
password.tip: If your password leaked elsewhere and you reuse it here, attackers get in silently.

suspicious.title: Verify it’s you
suspicious.subtitle: We detected unusual access. We sent a code to your recovery phone.
suspicious.tip: This is what Google actually does: a second factor on suspicious sign-ins. Never share the code.

mfa.title: Two-step verification
mfa.subtitle: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app.
mfa.tip: Authenticator apps (Authy, Google Authenticator, 1Password) are safer than SMS codes.

verify.title: You’re in!
verify.subtitle: You signed into your practice Google account.
verify.tip: Sign out on shared devices. Periodically review your account activity at myaccount.google.com.

current.title: Confirm your current password
current.subtitle: For security, we ask for your current password.
current.tip: If you’re asked for your current password and don’t know it, use "Forgot password".

new.title: Create a new password
new.subtitle: You can’t reuse a previous password.
new.tip: Change your password if you suspect a leak. Check haveibeenpwned.com with your email.

email.title: Recover your account
email.subtitle: Confirm your Google email and we’ll send a code.
email.tip: Phishers fake "recovery" pages to steal emails. Check the domain before typing anything.

code.title: Enter the code
code.subtitle: We sent a 6-digit code to your recovery email. It expires in 10 minutes.
code.tip: Recovery codes should be short-lived. The shorter the window, the smaller the attack surface.

scan.title: Turn on two-step verification
scan.subtitle: Scan this QR with your authenticator app.
scan.body: Open Google Authenticator, Authy or 1Password and scan this code. You can also copy the key manually.
scan.tip: The QR is real: scan it with your app and you’ll get valid codes. Useful for practicing.

verify.title: Confirm with a code
verify.subtitle: Enter the 6-digit code your app shows.
verify.tip: If the code fails, check your phone’s clock — TOTP codes depend on time.

disable.title: Turn off two-step verification
disable.subtitle: Confirm with your password before disabling.
disable.tip: Turning off 2FA reduces your security a lot. Only do it if you know what you’re doing.

password.label: Password
password.help: At least 8 characters. Mix letters, numbers and symbols.

name.title: Create a new account
name.subtitle: It's quick and easy.
name.tip: Use your real name only if you want people who know you to find you.

contact.title: What's your email?
contact.subtitle: We'll use it to log you in and recover your account.
contact.tip: Pick an email only you can access. If you lose that email, you lose the account.

password.title: Create a password
password.subtitle: Make it hard to guess and don't reuse it anywhere else.
password.tip: A unique password per account stops a leak elsewhere from spilling over here.

birthday.title: When were you born?
birthday.subtitle: Why we ask for your birthday.
birthday.tip: A real date helps you recover the account if it's stolen, but don't show it publicly on your profile.

gender.title: What's your gender?
gender.subtitle: You can change who sees this later.
gender.tip: Only share personal details with services you trust and always check who can see them.

email.title: Log in to Facebook
email.subtitle: Enter your email to continue.
email.tip: Check the URL before typing your email: facebook.com, with no extra letters or hyphens.

password.title: Welcome back
password.subtitle: Enter your password.
password.tip: Facebook will never ask for your password by message or email. If someone asks, it's phishing.

mfa.title: Two-factor verification
mfa.subtitle: Enter the code from your authenticator app.
mfa.tip: The code changes every 30 seconds and lives only on your phone. Never share it with anyone.

verify.title: You're in!
verify.subtitle: You're signed in on this device.
verify.tip: If you logged in on a borrowed device, sign out before you leave.

current.title: Confirm it's you
current.subtitle: Enter your current password to continue.
current.tip: If you don't remember your current password, go back and use 'Forgot password?' instead of guessing.

new.title: Create a new password
new.subtitle: Pick something unique you haven't used before.
new.tip: Change your password the moment you suspect anyone else knows it.

email.title: Recover your account
email.subtitle: Enter the email tied to your account.
email.tip: Facebook also lets you recover your account through trusted friends if you set that up beforehand.

code.title: Enter the code
code.subtitle: We sent a 6-digit code to your email.
code.tip: If you get a code you didn't request, someone is trying to break in. Change your password.

scan.title: Turn on two-factor
scan.subtitle: Scan the code with your authenticator app.
scan.body: Open your authenticator app and scan this code. If you can't, copy the key manually.
scan.tip: Two-factor is the single best protection against account takeover.

verify.title: Confirm the code
verify.subtitle: Enter the 6-digit code shown by your app.
verify.tip: If the app shows a different code each time, it's working correctly.

disable.title: Turn off two-factor
disable.subtitle: Your account will be less protected. Confirm with your password.
disable.tip: Only turn it off if you're switching methods. Without 2FA, one leaked password is enough to lose the account.

step1.label: Username
step1.tip: Verified accounts can also be hacked. Verification is not the same as security.

step2.label: Password
step2.tip: Reusing passwords is the most common mistake. A password manager solves this.

step3.label: 2FA Method
step3.tip: Physical security keys (like YubiKey) are the most secure method. SMS is better than nothing.

step4.label: Code
step4.tip: If someone asks for a code you just received, it's a real-time attack. Don't share it.

step1.label: Login
step1.tip: Accounts with followers are targets. More followers = more value for an attacker.

step2.label: Security settings
step2.tip: Check which apps have access to your account. Revoke any you don't recognize.

step3.label: 2FA Code
step3.tip: Enable 2FA even if you think your account isn't important. Attackers use them for spam.

step1.label: Bank login
step1.tip: Your bank will never send you a link by SMS to log in. Type the URL yourself.

step2.label: Device verification
step2.tip: New device alerts are your first line of defense. Don't ignore them.

step3.label: Security question
step3.tip: Don't use real answers. Invent answers that only you know.

step4.label: Confirm transaction
step4.tip: Always verify the amount and recipient before confirming. Scammers change details at the last moment.

step5.label: Summary
step5.tip: Review your statements regularly. Report any transaction you don't recognize.

runner.exitConfirm: Leave the simulation? Unsaved data will be lost.

username.label: Username
username.help: Lowercase letters, numbers, dots and underscores only.

contact.title: Create an Instagram account
contact.subtitle: We'll send a code to this email to verify it.
contact.tip: Check that the domain is instagram.com — not instagrarn.com or instagram-login.app.

name.title: What's your name?
name.subtitle: Your name may be visible to your contacts.
name.tip: The less personal info you share, the harder it is for someone to impersonate you.

username.title: Pick a username
username.subtitle: We suggest one; you can change it anytime.
username.tip: Your username is public; anyone trying to steal your account starts by finding it.

password.title: Create a password
password.subtitle: You need at least 8 characters.
password.tip: A strong password combined with 2FA is the best protection against account theft.

birthday.title: When's your birthday?
birthday.subtitle: This won't appear on your profile.
birthday.tip: Your real date helps account recovery; never share it with anyone who asks.

identity.title: Sign in
identity.subtitle: Enter your email or username.
identity.tip: If you got a link by message, check the domain before typing your details.

password.title: Your password
password.subtitle: Only you should know it.
password.tip: Instagram will never ask for your password by message, email or phone call.

mfa.title: Two-factor code
mfa.subtitle: Open your authentication app and enter the 6-digit code.
mfa.tip: The code changes every 30 seconds; don't share it with anyone.

verify.title: You're signed in
verify.subtitle: Welcome back.
verify.tip: If you see a sign-in you don't recognise, change your password immediately.

current.title: Confirm your current password
current.subtitle: To protect your account before the change.
current.tip: Change your password if you suspect anyone else knows it.

new.title: New password
new.subtitle: Choose one you haven't used before on this account.
new.tip: Reusing old passwords turns a single leak into many breaches.

identity.title: Recover your account
identity.subtitle: Enter your email or username and we'll send a code.
identity.tip: If the email doesn't arrive, check spam before asking for another code.

code.title: Enter the code
code.subtitle: We sent a 6-digit code to your email.
code.tip: Never share this code over chat, voice or a call — only type it here.

scan.title: Turn on two-factor
scan.subtitle: Scan this code with your authentication app.
scan.body: Open Google Authenticator, Authy or similar and scan the code. If you can't scan, copy the manual key.
scan.tip: Save the manual key somewhere safe to re-enable 2FA if you lose your phone.

verify.title: Confirm the code
verify.subtitle: Enter the 6-digit code your app shows.
verify.tip: If the code fails, check that your phone's time is set automatically.

disable.title: Disable 2FA
disable.subtitle: Confirm your password to continue.
disable.tip: Without 2FA, a leaked password is enough to enter your account.

password.label: Password
password.help: At least 12 characters. Mix uppercase, numbers and symbols.

nationalId.title: Open your SecureBank account
nationalId.subtitle: Let's start by verifying your identity.
nationalId.tip: Your bank will never ask for your password by email, SMS, call or WhatsApp.

name.title: What's your name?
name.subtitle: We'll use this name on your statements and communications.
name.tip: Use your legal name. Banks compare it with your government ID.

email.title: Your email address
email.subtitle: We'll send important alerts and security codes here.
email.tip: If you get an email with links to access your bank, don't click them. Always type the URL in your browser.

phone.title: Your mobile phone
phone.subtitle: We'll use it as an extra verification channel.
phone.tip: If someone calls saying they're from your bank and asks for codes, hang up and call the number on the back of your card.

password.title: Create a strong banking password
password.subtitle: At least 12 characters. Don't reuse it anywhere else.
password.tip: A strong, unique banking password protects your savings — never reuse it elsewhere.

totpScan.title: Set up your second factor
totpScan.subtitle: Scan this code with your authenticator app (Google Authenticator, Authy, 1Password).
totpScan.body: Open your authenticator app and add a new account by scanning this QR. If you can't scan, copy the manual key.
totpScan.tip: The authenticator code changes every 30 seconds. If anyone asks for it on the phone, hang up.

totpVerify.title: Verify the code
totpVerify.subtitle: Enter the 6-digit code shown by your app.
totpVerify.tip: Without this step we can't open the account. 2FA is mandatory because it protects your money.

email.title: Sign in to SecureBank
email.subtitle: Enter your email to continue.
email.tip: Always check that the URL starts with https:// and shows the correct domain before typing your password.

password.title: Welcome back
password.subtitle: Enter your password.
password.tip: If your password leaked elsewhere and you reuse it here, attackers could drain your account.

mfa.title: Two-step verification
mfa.subtitle: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app.
mfa.tip: The authenticator code changes every 30 seconds. If anyone asks for it on the phone, hang up.

verify.title: Signed in
verify.subtitle: You're now in your practice SecureBank account.
verify.tip: Sign out when you're done, especially on shared devices.

current.title: Confirm your current password
current.subtitle: For your security, we ask for it before changing.
current.tip: If you don't remember your password, use "Forgot your password?" instead of guessing.

mfa.title: Two-step verification
mfa.subtitle: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app.
mfa.tip: Your bank confirms sensitive changes with 2FA — this prevents attackers with your password from changing it.

new.title: New password
new.subtitle: You can't reuse previous passwords.
new.tip: A strong, unique banking password protects your savings — never reuse it elsewhere.

email.title: Recover access
email.subtitle: Enter the email tied to your account.
email.tip: If you receive a recovery email you didn't request, someone may be trying to access your account. Change your password.

code.title: Check your inbox
code.subtitle: We sent you a 6-digit code. It expires in 10 minutes.
code.tip: Your bank will never ask for your password by email, SMS, call or WhatsApp.

disable.title: Your bank doesn't allow disabling 2FA
disable.subtitle: For your security, banks always require two-step verification.
disable.tip: For safety, banks require 2FA always — it protects your money.

scan.title: Set up your second factor
scan.subtitle: Scan this code with your authenticator app.
scan.body: Open your authenticator app and add a new account by scanning this QR. If you can't scan, copy the manual key.
scan.tip: The authenticator code changes every 30 seconds. If anyone asks for it on the phone, hang up.

verify.title: Verify the code
verify.subtitle: Enter the 6-digit code shown by your app.
verify.tip: After enabling it, store your backup codes somewhere safe outside your phone.

auth.guestBanner: Sign in to save your progress across devices.

signInPage.title: Sign in
signInPage.subtitle: Save your progress and access from any device.
signInPage.privacyNote: We only use your unique ID. We don't store your email or name.

errorPage.title: Something went wrong
errorPage.subtitle: There was a problem signing in. Please try again.

roadmap.title: Learning path
roadmap.subtitle: Visualize your progress and discover what to learn next.

profile.title: My profile
profile.deleteWarning: This action is irreversible. All your data, progress, and badges will be deleted.
profile.noProgressYet: You have not completed any lesson yet. Start with whichever module interests you.

settings.title: Settings
settings.appearanceDesc: Choose between dark, light, or system theme.
settings.deleteDesc: This action is irreversible. All your data, progress and settings will be permanently deleted.
settings.simulatorsDesc: Manage your practice accounts for phishing simulators.
settings.privacyDesc: Information about what data we collect and how we use it.

coachMarks.roadmapDesc: See all modules and the recommended order here.
coachMarks.authAreaDesc: Sign in to sync your progress across all your devices.
coachMarks.passwordsCardDesc: The passwords module is the perfect starting point.

lesson.freshnessStale: This content is pending review. It still holds up, but may not reflect recent changes.
lesson.freshnessArchived: This content has not been reviewed in more than 18 months and may be out of date. Kept for reference while we update it.

certificates.earnedBody: If you want, you can issue a certificate under any display name. No account required. It is verifiable at a public link, with no email and no hidden identifiers.
certificates.displayNameHint: This appears verbatim on the public verification page. Use whatever name you want tied to this achievement — it does not have to be your real one.
certificates.error: Could not issue the certificate. Try again in a moment.

passwords.title: Strong passwords
passwords.description: You finished every lesson in the passwords track.

verifyPage.heading: Verifiable certificate
verifyPage.subheading: This page confirms the certificate was issued. We do not collect data on visitors.
verifyPage.ogDescription: Certificate issued by Safia to {name} for completing {track}.
verifyPage.revoked: This certificate has been revoked. The achievement stays on record but is no longer current.
verifyPage.privacyNote: This page does not log your IP or install cookies. Dates are shown with month granularity out of respect for the holder.

accounts.title: My accounts
accounts.emptySubtitle: Create your first practice account to get started.

picker.title: Which platform?
picker.subtitle: Pick a platform to create a new practice account.

email.taken: An account with that email already exists on this platform.

username.invalid: Only lowercase letters, numbers, periods and underscores (3-30 chars).

phone.invalid: Enter a valid phone number (include country code).

password.tooWeak: This password is too weak. Combine letters, numbers and symbols.
password.reused: You can't reuse a previous password.

code.expired: This code has expired. Request a new one.

account.notFound: No account found with that information.
